purpose: Test if A2 catches duplicate bug reports (different wording, same root issue)
tier_explanation: Standard difficulty — recognizable patterns + similar wording
source: Codex-generated alternative phrasings of REAL CPI bugs (cpi_duplicate_bug_reports.json)

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: SAG dropdown is not loading based on selected PMG
items.duplicate_description: While creating product data in Pimcore, after selecting the PMG, the SAG list is not filtered or populated as expected. The user has to proceed without the correct SAG mapping, which can create wrong product classification under the CPI PIM hierarchy.

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: PMG selection does not bring related SAG values
items.duplicate_description: In the product master form, SAG should depend on the chosen PMG. Currently after choosing PMG, the SAG field remains incorrect/blank, so Product Manager cannot select the valid SAG for the SKU or NPI data.

items.original_title: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.original_description: SAG should come on the selection of PMG
items.duplicate_title: SAG mapping missing after PMG is selected in PIM
items.duplicate_description: Observed in CPI Pimcore that the SAG values are not coming automatically once PMG is selected. This affects Product Master Group wise data entry because SAG is needed before routing the product further in the workflow.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description:
e.g. Created a New NPI and it is under the coated Industrial > belt

But Mail are go to Different Product Developers
Pandu_B -- Bonded Industrial. >Thinwheel, Thinwheel G2
Gopalakrishnan_C - Coated Industrial > Sheets

Mathews_Reji -- Coated Retail > Sheets Retail
items.duplicate_title: NPI approval mail going to wrong Product Developer for selected PMG
items.duplicate_description: Created a New NPI under Coated Industrial > Belt, but the notification did not go to the Product Developer mapped to that PMG/SAG. Pimcore is routing the email to users from other PMG groups like Bonded Industrial and Coated Retail.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description:
e.g. Created a New NPI and it is under the coated Industrial > belt

But Mail are go to Different Product Developers
Pandu_B -- Bonded Industrial. >Thinwheel, Thinwheel G2
Gopalakrishnan_C - Coated Industrial > Sheets

Mathews_Reji -- Coated Retail > Sheets Retail
items.duplicate_title: Incorrect email recipient mapping in New NPI workflow
items.duplicate_description: For NPI creation, email trigger should follow the selected PMG in PIM. Actual result is that Product Developers from different PMG categories are receiving the email, which can cause wrong team approval for the product.

items.original_title: Mail are not trigger as per the PMG selected in NPI.
items.original_description:
e.g. Created a New NPI and it is under the coated Industrial > belt

But Mail are go to Different Product Developers
Pandu_B -- Bonded Industrial. >Thinwheel, Thinwheel G2
Gopalakrishnan_C - Coated Industrial > Sheets

Mathews_Reji -- Coated Retail > Sheets Retail
items.duplicate_title: PMG based NPI notification rule is not working
items.duplicate_description: When a new NPI is created for a specific PMG, the mail distribution is not respecting the PMG/Product Developer mapping. The same issue is seen for Coated Industrial item where mail is sent to unrelated PMG owners.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer sees SKU Enrichment Done instead of Approve, Product Manager sees Approve instead of SKU Enrichment Done, generic Update appears where it should not, Product Head button label is inconsistent, and Product Manager does not see SKU Enrichment Done After Update after Product Head sends update.
items.duplicate_title: Wrong role-wise actions visible during SKU update flow
items.duplicate_description: In CPI Pimcore SKU update workflow, the action dropdown is not matching the workflow matrix. Product Developer and Product Manager are seeing swapped actions, and Product Head is getting inconsistent update button labels.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer sees SKU Enrichment Done instead of Approve, Product Manager sees Approve instead of SKU Enrichment Done, generic Update appears where it should not, Product Head button label is inconsistent, and Product Manager does not see SKU Enrichment Done After Update after Product Head sends update.
items.duplicate_title: SKU update workflow buttons not following approval sequence
items.duplicate_description: During SKU update testing, the workflow actions are confusing because users are getting actions for another stage. Product Manager is not getting SKU Enrichment Done After Update after Product Head sends it back, so the SKU cannot be processed as per expected CPI flow.

items.original_title: Incorrect Action Buttons Displayed in SKU Update Workflow
items.original_description: The action button dropdown menus are not displaying the correct options for each user role in SKU Update workflow. Product Developer sees SKU Enrichment Done instead of Approve, Product Manager sees Approve instead of SKU Enrichment Done, generic Update appears where it should not, Product Head button label is inconsistent, and Product Manager does not see SKU Enrichment Done After Update after Product Head sends update.
items.duplicate_title: Incorrect action dropdown options for SKU roles
items.duplicate_description: Role permission issue observed in PIM for SKU Update. Product Developer, Product Manager, and Product Head are not seeing only their allowed workflow buttons, which can move SKU to the wrong status.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: Reopening product creates an extra SKU with ERP and DAM tabs
items.duplicate_description: When Product Manager reopens an existing product in Pimcore, system creates a new SKU instead of keeping the same product/SKU structure. The newly created SKU is also showing ERP and DAM tabs, which should not appear in this state.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: Duplicate SKU generated after product reopen action
items.duplicate_description: On using Reopen Product workflow, a fresh SKU node is created under the product. This looks like incorrect object creation because ERP and DAM tabs are also visible under that SKU after reopen.

items.original_title: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.original_description: After Reopen the Product, New SKU is created. Under the SKU, ERP and DAM tab are showing.
items.duplicate_title: ERP/DAM tabs appearing on SKU created from reopen flow
items.duplicate_description: After reopening a product, the PIM tree shows a new SKU and the SKU detail page contains ERP and DAM tabs. Expected is that reopen should not create a new SKU or expose ERP/DAM tabs at SKU level.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When the Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed / marking does not enable the transition, Reopen product workflow and Approve buttons disappear, and approving again shows two statuses simultaneously: InReview and Product Data Approved.
items.duplicate_title: Reopen product action breaks due to missing email template
items.duplicate_description: Product Manager is unable to reopen product workflow in CPI. First click on Reopen product workflow gives email sending failed because template path is empty, and after retry the workflow transition also fails.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When the Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed / marking does not enable the transition, Reopen product workflow and Approve buttons disappear, and approving again shows two statuses simultaneously: InReview and Product Data Approved.
items.duplicate_title: Product workflow shows two statuses after failed reopen
items.duplicate_description: After trying to reopen a product in Pimcore, system throws workflow errors and later shows both InReview and Product Data Approved for the same product. This is causing status mismatch for PIM approval tracking.

items.original_title: Reopen Product Workflow Fails - Email Error & Conflicting Statuses
items.original_description: When the Product Manager attempts to reopen a product workflow, first attempt fails with email template path is empty, retry fails with transition failed / marking does not enable the transition, Reopen product workflow and Approve buttons disappear, and approving again shows two statuses simultaneously: InReview and Product Data Approved.
items.duplicate_title: Reopen Product workflow option disappears after transition error
items.duplicate_description: In Product Manager login, Reopen product workflow is failing and then the action buttons are not available properly. It appears the failed email/transition is leaving the product workflow in an invalid state.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow, SKU belongs to Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail. After Product Developer approves and sends to Product Manager, email is incorrectly sent to DISC Manager instead of Product Manager, mail body does not show post approval user Product Head, mail/notification also goes to Product Head Industrial, and clicking notification in Product Head Industrial shows error.
items.duplicate_title: Change SKU notification sent to wrong manager and wrong Product Head
items.duplicate_description: For Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail SKU, Change SKU approval mail is not going only to Product Manager Retail. DISC Manager and Product Head Industrial are receiving notification, which is incorrect for this PMG/SAG routing.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow, SKU belongs to Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail. After Product Developer approves and sends to Product Manager, email is incorrectly sent to DISC Manager instead of Product Manager, mail body does not show post approval user Product Head, mail/notification also goes to Product Head Industrial, and clicking notification in Product Head Industrial shows error.
items.duplicate_title: Post approval user missing in Change SKU email body
items.duplicate_description: In Change SKU workflow, after Product Developer approval, the generated email does not show the next post approval user as Product Head. Also notification is sent to an unrelated Industrial user and opens with error.

items.original_title: Incorrect email notifications and missing post approval user in Change SKU workflow
items.original_description: In Production Change SKU workflow, SKU belongs to Bonded Retail > Super Abrasive Retail. After Product Developer approves and sends to Product Manager, email is incorrectly sent to DISC Manager instead of Product Manager, mail body does not show post approval user Product Head, mail/notification also goes to Product Head Industrial, and clicking notification in Product Head Industrial shows error.
items.duplicate_title: Retail SKU approval mail is routed to unrelated users
items.duplicate_description: SKU is under Bonded Retail, but Pimcore notification is triggering to DISC Manager and Product Head Industrial. Expected mail should go to mapped Product Manager Retail and the email should mention correct next approver.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains the line 'Post approval please send ALO RESIN SANDER DISC test to Product Manager for review.' This is misleading because after DAM Manager approval the product is directly Published to Web.
items.duplicate_title: DAM Manager email template still asks to send product to PM
items.duplicate_description: After Marketing Manager sends product to DAM Manager, the DAM approval mail contains an extra instruction to send product to Product Manager. In actual CPI workflow, DAM Manager approval should move product to Publish to Web, so this line should not be present.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains the line 'Post approval please send ALO RESIN SANDER DISC test to Product Manager for review.' This is misleading because after DAM Manager approval the product is directly Published to Web.
items.duplicate_title: Wrong Product Manager review text in DAM approval mail
items.duplicate_description: DAM Manager received email with Product Manager review instruction even though product is already at DAM stage. This creates confusion in DAM workflow because Product Manager should not be involved after DAM approval.

items.original_title: Email shows incorrect line after DAM Manager approval - Product Manager reference not required
items.original_description: In Production workflow Marketing Manager to DAM Manager to Publish to Web, DAM Manager approval email incorrectly contains the line 'Post approval please send ALO RESIN SANDER DISC test to Product Manager for review.' This is misleading because after DAM Manager approval the product is directly Published to Web.
items.duplicate_title: Publish to Web email content has incorrect next-step message
items.duplicate_description: In Pimcore Prod, DAM workflow email body is showing an outdated next-step line mentioning Product Manager. Expected email should only tell DAM Manager to review/publish the product to web.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In the ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates. Date picker should allow only current date or past dates.
items.duplicate_title: ERP form date picker allows future dates
items.duplicate_description: In ERP form PIM ID filter, Start Date and End Date calendar is allowing dates greater than today. Since ERP/PIM records cannot exist for future creation dates, the filter gives empty result and confuses the user.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In the ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates. Date picker should allow only current date or past dates.
items.duplicate_title: Future date validation missing in ERP PIM ID search
items.duplicate_description: Observed that ERP support user can select future dates while filtering PIM IDs. Pimcore should block future date selection in both fields and allow only today or previous dates.

items.original_title: Restrict Future Date Selection in ERP Form Date Filters
items.original_description: In the ERP form, users are able to select future dates in Start Date and End Date fields. This returns no PIM IDs because no PIM IDs are created with future dates. Date picker should allow only current date or past dates.
items.duplicate_title: Start and End date fields in ERP filter need max date restriction
items.duplicate_description: The ERP tab filter accepts future Start Date/End Date values, resulting in no PIM ID listing. Expected behavior is to restrict the date control up to current date only.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: As per client attribute mapping sheet, ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: Extra ERP fields showing after product is published
items.duplicate_description: During New NPI creation, ERP attributes are matching the mapping sheet, but after publish and selecting New item code creation, five additional ERP fields appear. The post-publish ERP view should not add fields outside client mapping.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: As per client attribute mapping sheet, ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: ERP attribute list differs between NPI and published product
items.duplicate_description: In CPI PIM, the ERP section is not consistent across New NPI and published product flow. After publish, enabling new item code creation shows extra attributes that are not present during NPI setup.

items.original_title: Mismatch of ERP Attributes Between New NPI Creation and Post-Publish View
items.original_description: As per client attribute mapping sheet, ERP form should display consistent attributes during New NPI creation and after product publish. After publishing product and enabling New item code creation, ERP form displays five extra attributes not required by the client sheet.
items.duplicate_title: New item code creation exposes unmapped ERP attributes
items.duplicate_description: When product is published, ERP form with New item code creation checkbox displays five fields beyond the agreed attribute sheet. This can lead ERP team to maintain unnecessary values.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: ERP tab becomes non-clickable after opening DAM tab
items.duplicate_description: Once ERP approves the product, if any user opens the DAM tab, the ERP tab stops responding. This is seen across roles and blocks users from returning to ERP data in Pimcore.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: DAM tab navigation is disabling ERP tab after approval
items.duplicate_description: For ERP approved product, tab navigation has issue. Clicking DAM tab makes ERP tab unclickable for Product Manager/Product Head and other users, so ERP form cannot be reviewed again.

items.original_title: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.original_description: After ERP Approved the Product, If user click on DAM Tab then ERP tab can't be clickable in all the User.
items.duplicate_title: ERP tab locked after DAM tab click on approved product
items.duplicate_description: In product detail screen, after ERP approval the user can click DAM tab, but after that ERP tab is not accessible. Expected both ERP and DAM tabs should remain clickable as per role permission.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]: Please choose a valid option for select/multiselect field [SAGDescription]. Current value: YS592-Ceramic BELTS. The value exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG, suggesting dropdown/master data mismatch.
items.duplicate_title: Valid BELT SAGDescription rejected when NPI moves to Coated
items.duplicate_description: After ERP item code update, the NPI product is shifted to Coated section but Pimcore throws invalid option for SAGDescription YS592-Ceramic BELTS. This SAG value is available in data model for BELT PMG, so system dropdown mapping looks out of sync.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]: Please choose a valid option for select/multiselect field [SAGDescription]. Current value: YS592-Ceramic BELTS. The value exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG, suggesting dropdown/master data mismatch.
items.duplicate_title: SAGDescription master value not recognized during product movement
items.duplicate_description: Product approval flow is blocked after ERP adds item code because SAGDescription is treated as invalid. The selected value belongs to BELT PMG and exists in master sheet, but PIM select field does not accept it.

items.original_title: Error Showing After Product Approval Opening, Due to Invalid SAGDescription value error showing while opening the item.
items.original_description: When creating a new NPI and ERP team adds item code, the product moves from NPI section to Coated section and throws Invalid Option field [SAGDescription]: Please choose a valid option for select/multiselect field [SAGDescription]. Current value: YS592-Ceramic BELTS. The value exists in Data Model sheet under BELT PMG, suggesting dropdown/master data mismatch.
items.duplicate_title: Invalid option error for SAGDescription after NPI approval
items.duplicate_description: Opening the approved product shows invalid select/multiselect option error for SAGDescription. Root appears to be mismatch between Data Model SAG values and configured Pimcore options for the PMG.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: DAM tab visible before product approval for PM/PH/MM
items.duplicate_description: For Product Manager, Product Head, and Marketing Manager roles, DAM tab should not be shown until the product is approved. Currently the DAM tab is available before approval, which is not matching the CPI permission matrix.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: Pre-approval products are exposing DAM section
items.duplicate_description: In Pimcore product workflow, DAM tab is visible too early for PM, PH, and MM users. DAM should appear only after product approval and then follow role-wise access rules.

items.original_title: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for PM, PH, MM. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.original_description: Remove DAM tab till the product is approved for pm, ph, mm. once the product is approved then only dam tab should be visible as per permissions
items.duplicate_title: Role-wise DAM tab visibility not restricted before approval
items.duplicate_description: Observed that users can see DAM tab even before product approval stage. Expected behavior is to hide DAM for Product Manager/Product Head/Marketing Manager until the product is approved.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: Product Manager cannot see DAM asset thumbnail
items.duplicate_description: When Product Manager opens the DAM tab for a product, the uploaded asset image/thumbnail is not displayed. DAM data is present but the preview is missing for PM user.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: DAM tab image preview blank for PM role
items.duplicate_description: In CPI Pimcore, Product Manager login does not show the product asset thumbnail under DAM tab. This makes it difficult for PM to verify the digital asset linked to the SKU/product.

items.original_title: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.original_description: Asset image/ Thumbnail are not Shown in DAM Tab For Product Manager User.
items.duplicate_title: Asset thumbnail not rendering in DAM section for Product Manager
items.duplicate_description: For Product Manager role, DAM tab opens but the image thumbnail area is blank. Expected uploaded DAM asset preview should be visible so PM can confirm correct product image.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: Product Developer gets DAM access after publish
items.duplicate_description: After DAM Manager publishes product to web, Product Developer login starts showing the DAM tab. Product Developer should not view or edit DAM fields in any stage as per CPI role access.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: DAM tab permission opens incorrectly for Product Developer
items.duplicate_description: In Prod, a published product exposes DAM tab to Product Developer. This is a role permission regression because Product Developer should not have DAM visibility even after product is published.

items.original_title: DAM Tab should be invisible for Product Developer, but is showing after product publish
items.original_description: In Production Pimcore, for Product Developer role and Published to Web status, DAM Tab should remain invisible or disabled before and after publish. After DAM Manager publishes the product, DAM Tab becomes visible to Product Developer and user can view/add values in DAM fields.
items.duplicate_title: Post-publish DAM fields editable by Product Developer
items.duplicate_description: Product Developer can see and add values in DAM tab after product publish. Expected is DAM tab should remain hidden/disabled for this role both before and after Publish to Web.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, open and delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: Blank popup shown on second SKU ERP import
items.duplicate_description: After deleting imported SKUs and importing the same SKU ERP file again under same parent product, the import succeeds but Pimcore shows an empty message popup. User cannot understand whether SKU import passed or failed.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, open and delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: No success message when re-importing deleted SKUs
items.duplicate_description: SKU ERP import gives blank notification on second attempt for the same parent product. The two SKUs are created successfully, but message text is missing in the popup.

items.original_title: Blank message in SKU ERP import in 2 attempt
items.original_description: Import two new SKUs successfully in new product, open and delete the SKUs but not the parent product, then import the same two SKUs again in the same parent product. A blank message popup is shown but the two SKUs are successfully imported.
items.duplicate_title: Second import of same SKU file completes with empty alert
items.duplicate_description: Steps followed: import SKU file, delete child SKUs only, then import same file again. Actual result is blank popup while import is successful; expected proper success/failure message for SKU ERP import.

items.original_title: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.original_description: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.duplicate_title: Imported SKU records missing from Search and Export
items.duplicate_description: After bulk SKU import in Pimcore, the created SKUs are available under product but not appearing in Search & Export screen. Search result should include imported SKU records for export validation.

items.original_title: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.original_description: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.duplicate_title: Bulk uploaded SKUs not searchable in export module
items.duplicate_description: SKU data created through bulk/import process is not visible in Search and Export window. This blocks QA from verifying imported SKU values and generating export for business users.

items.original_title: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.original_description: BULK / Imported data SKU is not visible in Search& Export window
items.duplicate_title: Search & Export does not list imported SKU data
items.duplicate_description: In CPI PIM, SKUs added by bulk import should be returned in the Search & Export grid. Currently imported SKUs are missing from the listing, while manually maintained data appears.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Product API returns PIM data without auth token
items.duplicate_description: Product API is exposing CPI product/SKU data even when request is sent without authorization. API should reject unauthenticated calls instead of returning product master data.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Unauthorized access possible on Product API endpoint
items.duplicate_description: While testing API security, Product API response is coming without login/token. This is a security issue because ERP or external users should not get PIM product data without valid authorization.

items.original_title: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.original_description: Security Risk in Product API, without authorization data is coming
items.duplicate_title: Missing authorization check for Product API
items.duplicate_description: CPI Product API does not seem to validate authorization header. Without valid auth, API still returns product records, which can expose SKU/PMG/SAG details outside allowed access.

items.original_title: Issue in Export functionality(Search and export)
items.original_description: In Search and Export, select Retail vertical and click Search. 300 records are shown. After clicking Export button, only 10 records are showing and records are page wise. Expected after clicking export, all records should be shown in the grid.
items.duplicate_title: Search export returns only current page records
items.duplicate_description: In Search & Export module, Retail search shows 300 records but export action displays only 10 records. Export should include all search results, not only the first page.

items.original_title: Issue in Export functionality(Search and export)
items.original_description: In Search and Export, select Retail vertical and click Search. 300 records are shown. After clicking Export button, only 10 records are showing and records are page wise. Expected after clicking export, all records should be shown in the grid.
items.duplicate_title: Export grid is limited to 10 Retail records
items.duplicate_description: After searching Retail vertical in CPI Pimcore, total result count is 300. On clicking export, only page-wise 10 records are shown, so exported data is incomplete.

items.original_title: Issue in Export functionality(Search and export)
items.original_description: In Search and Export, select Retail vertical and click Search. 300 records are shown. After clicking Export button, only 10 records are showing and records are page wise. Expected after clicking export, all records should be shown in the grid.
items.duplicate_title: Search and Export not exporting full result set
items.duplicate_description: The export functionality is taking only visible page records from the grid. For Retail vertical all 300 matching product/SKU records should be included after export button click.
